sentence_reviews.text: Parameters are chosen to assign high probability to observed targets or data.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, probability-modeling and maximum-likelihood treatment; book landing page identifies the source as a probabilistic ML textbook, lines 21-29 and endorsements lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Maximum likelihood is exactly the procedure of choosing parameters that make observed data probable.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy is to connect Probability theory, Information theory to downstream capabilities including Linear models & generalized linear models, Hidden Markov models & CRFs, Matrix factorization & topic models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, broad coverage of probabilistic ML and foundations; landing page lines 45-48 describe statistics, information theory, and traditional/modern ML coverage.
sentence_reviews.notes: The source supports MLE/cross-entropy as foundational, but the exact graph bridge and downstream list are atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Linear models & generalized linear models
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, supervised/probabilistic models and classical ML coverage; landing page endorsements lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: MLE is a standard training principle for many GLMs, but the 'substrate for' phrasing is graph terminology.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Hidden Markov models & CRFs
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, probabilistic ML framing; landing page lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: HMMs and CRFs use likelihood or conditional likelihood objectives; exact downstream relationship is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Matrix factorization & topic models
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, probabilistic ML framing; landing page lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Likelihood objectives support probabilistic matrix factorization and topic models, but the inspected source location does not directly establish this exact example.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Information theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, foundations include probability and information theory; landing page lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: The dependencies are plausible, but the surrogate-objective failure wording is a general caution not directly supported by the inspected source location.

sentence_reviews.text: Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, probabilistic ML source identification lines 21-29 and foundation description lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Likelihood is a probability of observed data under a parameterized model.

sentence_reviews.text: Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy uses Information theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, endorsement notes grounding in information theory, lines 45-46.
sentence_reviews.notes: Cross-entropy is an information-theoretic quantity; the source supports the foundation but not this exact dependency wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, ML methods coverage lines 45-48.
sentence_reviews.notes: Cross-entropy is commonly used in supervised classification, but MLE is broader than supervised learning and does not require it.

sentence_reviews.text: Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy uses Generative modeling as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction, probabilistic ML source identification lines 21-29.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generative models often use MLE, but MLE does not use generative modeling as an ingredient; direction may be overstated.

edge_reviews.notes: Probability is required to define likelihoods of observed data.
Cross-entropy is information-theoretic, but maximum likelihood can be stated without information theory.
The dependency is common for classification loss, not intrinsic to maximum likelihood in general.
Generative models commonly depend on likelihood objectives; the reverse direction is not generally required.
